role_cards.briefing: Ms. Chen's arrival is delayed by approximately 90 minutes due to a flight connection delay at JFK. Revised arrival is now 5:30–6:00 PM Thursday. Adjust the welcome timing; ensure the room is still ready and holds. Do not call or message her during the delay — she knows. Have everything ready silently.
priority_actions: Confirm garden-view King suite held and ready for 5:30 PM arrival
Notify Asaya Spa that the 4 PM hold is released; Thursday spa unlikely now
Have still water and light fruit plate timed for 6 PM room readiness
Note Friday morning cycling intent is unchanged — Bluejay Bikes still on
suppressed: Any arrival activity push — she is arriving tired and later than planned

role_cards.briefing: Delay does not affect Friday morning cycling plan. Bluejay Bikes is still confirmed for 6:30–6:45 AM Friday. Thursday evening is now very short — Ridge Rosé Reveal can be offered but keep it brief and low-key. She may simply want dinner and sleep.
priority_actions: Bluejay Bikes Friday 6:30 AM — confirm unchanged
Route card for Old La Honda and Portola Valley loop — ready at check-in
Offer Ridge Rosé Reveal as a 45-minute quiet option after check-in, not a full evening
Have a quiet Madera table available for late dinner around 7:30 PM
suppressed: Friday Nights at Madera (Thursday arrival is now too late to suggest for this evening)
Anything requiring more than 60 minutes of energy on Thursday evening

role_cards.briefing: Thursday 4 PM hold is released due to the delay. Asaya Thursday is off the table. Brief the team: if Ms. Chen asks about spa access on arrival, offer the garden walk-in only — no booked treatment tonight. Friday post-ride slot may be relevant; hold a 9:30 AM option.
priority_actions: Release Thursday 4 PM hold
Hold a Friday 9:30 AM recovery treatment slot post-cycling
Outdoor garden available Thursday evening if she wants quiet air

role_cards.briefing: Revised dinner timing: hold a quiet Madera garden table for 7:30 PM Thursday. In-suite breakfast Friday 7:00 AM unchanged. Ridge Rosé Reveal available Thursday evening but at low intensity — 45 minutes maximum, no extended tasting format given the late arrival.
priority_actions: Madera garden table held for 7:30 PM Thursday
In-suite breakfast Friday 7:00 AM (FT, WSJ, New Yorker) — unchanged
Ridge Rosé Reveal: brief the wine team for a 45-minute quiet pour, not the full event format
suppressed: Extended wine tasting on Thursday evening

role_cards.briefing: Turndown adjusted to 8:00 PM given later arrival. Friday morning in-suite breakfast unchanged at 7:00 AM. No interruptions after 9 PM.
priority_actions: Turndown by 8:00 PM Thursday (adjusted from earlier window)
In-suite breakfast Friday 7:00 AM — unchanged
No hallway noise or interruptions Thursday night

role_cards.briefing: Delay-adjusted plan: the cycling and wine thread remain intact; the arrival is shorter than ideal but the Friday arc is preserved. The key adjustment is Thursday evening compression — everything is dialed back to quiet landing mode. Do not over-program. The handwritten note in the room should acknowledge the long travel day without referencing the delay specifically.
priority_actions: Handwritten note in room — acknowledge travel, don't reference delay
Bluejay Bikes Friday 6:30 AM confirmed — this is the day's anchor
Ridge Rosé Reveal available tonight if she wants it; no push
suppressed: Any reference to the delay in person

suppression: Any Thursday evening programming beyond quiet dinner and optional 45-minute Ridge Rosé Reveal
References to the flight delay in direct guest interaction
guest_message: Welcome to Rosewood, Ms. Chen — we hope the journey wasn't too rough. Your garden suite is ready. A quiet table at Madera is held for 7:30 if you'd like dinner, and the Ridge Rosé is available whenever you're settled. Tomorrow morning: Bluejay Bikes is confirmed for 6:30 AM and your route card is at the desk. Rest well.
